title: validate-injection-rules.mjs: catch duplicate Input_*/Output_* titles
description:
Follow-up to the wan22_i2v Low-LoRA fix (`c21c1202`).

PROBLEM: the app injects BY TITLE (models.js injectionPrefix, e.g. 'Lora_Low'). Two nodes sharing a title means only one resolves and the rest silently drop their value. Check 4 (reachability) is blind to this: duplicate-titled nodes are all properly wired, so every one of them reaches a capture node. Nothing errors, the user picks a LoRA, the model never sees it.

This shipped undetected in wan22_i2v (nodes 793-798 all titled Input_Lora_Low_1) and is STILL LIVE in wan22_t2v + wan22_t2v_stage2 (same six nodes, same collision) — confirmed on master as well, so both families shipped broken.

FIX (agent): add a duplicate-title check to scripts/validate-injection-rules.mjs. Corpus survey shows the ONLY duplicates anywhere are these wan22_t2v ones, so the rule can be strict — no legitimate reuse to exempt. Cover Output_* too: numbered captures (Output_Image_2/_3) collide the same way on read-back.

FIX (USER — graph edit, agents never edit workflow JSON): retitle wan22_t2v nodes 794-798 to Input_Lora_Low_2.._6 in the raw template, matching what was already done for i2v, then `node scripts/sync-raw-workflows.mjs`.

VERIFY: the new check FAILS on wan22_t2v.json before the graph fix and passes after; the already-fixed i2v twin passes; the rest of the corpus stays green.

DONE 2026-07-18. Validator check shipped in `e4054df6` (Input_*/Output_* only, case-insensitive, no exemptions). User retitled wan22_t2v nodes 794-798 to _2.._6; resynced — only the t2v family rebuilt, both generated files now carry unique titles. Full corpus sweep is clean apart from the pre-existing, unrelated 'Model Merger.json has no capture node'. Changelog entry corrected: the earlier wording wrongly claimed t2v was unaffected; both i2v and t2v had the collision and both are fixed. Master intentionally NOT backported (user call) — 1.2.0 carries the fix.
